question: Is the tablecloth color changed to a rich hunter green?
Is the red-and-white gingham pattern completely removed from the tablecloth?
Does the tablecloth texture resemble velvet (e.g., soft sheen, short pile, lack of coarse weave)?
Does the tablecloth appear to be a solid color without any residual grid lines or check patterns?
Is the new material applied consistently across the entire table, including in small gaps between dishes and arms?
Are all people (faces, clothing, Santa hats) preserved unchanged?
Are the guests' hands and arms resting on or near the table preserved without green color bleeding?
Are the food items (turkey, yule log cake) and dishes on the table preserved?
Is the Christmas tree and its decorations on the right side unchanged?
Is the fireplace area (stone wall, fire, stockings, wreath) on the left side unchanged?
Does the lighting on the velvet fabric match the warm ambient lighting of the room (e.g., reflections from the fire)?
Are the boundaries between the tablecloth and foreground objects (dishes, arms) clean and realistic?
Is the edited image free from unnatural blurring, warping, or resolution loss?
